summary: Camille was involved in Zoé being playful with the material, and she was attentive overall. She knew parts of the story, understood the worksheet activity, and loved the Present for me activity.

Camille could recite the story partially. They all got bored by the end of it. They all could do the worksheet activity about what Oliver and Baby could or couldn’t do very well. They needed my help to remind the correct structures for a lot of the activities, but they did show a real understanding and a certain memorisation of the vocabulary. They loved the Who’s missing activity.

Camille was a bit distracting with Zoé, but it was alright. The group also did very well on the worksheet activity, loved a present for me and the Dice Game, and they are still learning to ask questions about the person's physical appearance before just asking who it is. It is a work in progress.
Camille was not always giving the material back directly when asked, and was hiding it jokingly. The group loved the activities, including "A present for me," Family Bingo, and Dice Game.

Camille was very attentive. She could remember the vocabulary words very well, tell the colors and the clothing items very well, and could remember some prepositions. She was very happy to listen to the story and seemed to really enjoy the new story.

Camille seemed to really enjoy the new story. She could repeat when I asked her to repeat, quickly understood the new weather and action miming routine, and quickly understood the new mood routine with 'he's smiling because he's happy.' She was a bit less confident today and seemed more hesitant, but I kept encouraging her and I think she felt quite happy. I was really proud of their behaviour.
